description: IT Onboarding Automation - Complete Workflow Definitions

itsm_create_ticket.description: Create a new service ticket in the ITSM portal

steps.name: navigate_to_itsm
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{itsm_url}}

steps.name: click_create_ticket
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Ticket' button to open the ticket creation form

steps.name: fill_ticket_form
submit_ticket
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Ticket' button at the bottom of the form to submit it. Wait for the success message to appear.

steps.name: read_ticket_id
steps.instruction: Look at the success message popup. Find the ticket ID which starts with 'INC-' followed by 6 digits. Return ONLY the ticket ID (example format: INC-123456).
steps.description: Read and return the ticket ID from success message

steps.name: close_popup
steps.instruction: Click the OK button to close the success message popup.

itsm_update_status.description: Update ticket status in ITSM portal

steps.name: navigate_to_itsm
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{itsm_url}}

steps.name: clear_filters
steps.instruction: Click the 'Clear Filters' button if visible to reset the ticket list

steps.name: search_ticket
steps.instruction: Clear the search field if it has any text, then type '{{ticket_id}}' into the search field. Wait for the table to filter.

steps.name: check_results
steps.instruction: Look at the Service Tickets table. Count how many rows are visible. If there are 0 rows, say 'NO RESULTS'. If there are 1 or more rows, say 'FOUND RESULTS'.

steps.name: click_update
steps.instruction: Look at the tickets table. Find the FIRST row. In that row, find the 'Update' button in the Actions column and click it.

steps.name: select_status
steps.instruction: A modal dialog titled 'Update Ticket Status' should now be visible. Find the radio button labeled '{{new_status}}' and click it to select it.

steps.name: confirm_update
steps.instruction: In the status update modal, click the 'Update Status' button to confirm and save the change.

inventory_search.description: Search for an item in the inventory portal and check stock level

steps.name: navigate_to_inventory
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{inventory_url}}

steps.name: clear_filters
steps.instruction: Click the 'Clear Filters' button if visible

steps.name: search_item
steps.instruction: In the search field, enter '{{item_name}}' and wait for the table to filter

steps.name: read_stock_level
steps.instruction: Look at the inventory table. Find the row that contains '{{item_name}}'. Read the 'Available' column value for that row. If no matching row is found, return '0'. Return ONLY the number.
steps.description: Read available stock quantity from filtered results

procurement_create_po.description: Create a purchase order in the procurement portal

steps.name: navigate_to_procurement
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{procurement_url}}

steps.name: click_create_po
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Purchase Order' button

steps.name: select_vendor
steps.instruction: Select '{{vendor}}' from the 'Vendor' dropdown

steps.name: enter_item
steps.instruction: In the 'Item' field, clear any existing text and enter: {{item_name}}

steps.name: enter_quantity
steps.instruction: In the 'Quantity' field, clear and enter: {{quantity}}

steps.name: enter_price
steps.instruction: In the 'Unit Price' field, clear and enter: {{unit_price}}

steps.name: enter_budget_code
steps.instruction: In the 'Budget Code' field, clear and enter: {{budget_code}}

steps.name: submit_po
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create PO' button to submit the form

steps.name: read_po_id
steps.instruction: Look at the success message or the Purchase Orders table. Find the PO number that was just created (format: PO- followed by year and 3 digits). Return ONLY that PO ID value.

steps.name: navigate_to_procurement
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{procurement_url}}

steps.name: select_po
steps.instruction: Find the row with PO number '{{po_id}}' and check its checkbox

steps.name: click_submit
steps.instruction: Click the 'Submit Selected' button

steps.name: navigate_to_procurement
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{procurement_url}}

steps.name: select_po
steps.instruction: Find the row with PO number '{{po_id}}' and check its checkbox

steps.name: click_approve
steps.instruction: Click the 'Approve Selected' button

procurement_receive.description: Mark a purchase order as delivered

steps.name: navigate_to_procurement
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{procurement_url}}

steps.name: receive_delivery
steps.instruction: Find the row with PO number '{{po_id}}'. Click the 'Receive Delivery' button in that row.

inventory_add_item.description: Add a new item to inventory after delivery

steps.name: navigate_to_inventory
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{inventory_url}}

steps.name: click_add_item
steps.instruction: Click the 'Add Item' button

steps.name: enter_name
steps.instruction: In the 'Item Name' field, enter: {{name}}

steps.name: select_category
steps.instruction: Select '{{category}}' from the 'Category' dropdown

steps.name: enter_manufacturer
steps.instruction: In the 'Manufacturer' field, enter: {{manufacturer}}

steps.name: enter_model
steps.instruction: In the 'Model' field, enter: {{model}}

steps.name: enter_stock_level
steps.instruction: In the 'Stock Level' field, enter: {{stock_level}}

steps.name: enter_unit_cost
steps.instruction: In the 'Unit Cost' field, enter: {{unit_cost}}

steps.name: enter_location
steps.instruction: In the 'Location' field, enter: {{location}}

steps.name: submit_item
steps.instruction: Click the 'Add Item' button to submit the form

inventory_allocate.description: Allocate inventory item to employee via browser

steps.name: navigate_to_inventory
steps.instruction: Navigate to {{inventory_url}}

steps.name: clear_filters
steps.instruction: Click the 'Clear Filters' button if visible to reset the inventory list

steps.name: search_item
steps.instruction: In the search field, enter '{{item_name}}' to filter the inventory list

steps.name: select_item
steps.instruction: In the inventory table, find the row containing '{{item_name}}' and check its checkbox in the first column

steps.name: click_allocate
steps.instruction: Click the 'Allocate Item' button (it should now show '1 selected') to open the allocation form

steps.name: fill_allocation_form
submit_allocation
steps.instruction: Click the 'Allocate Item' button at the bottom of the modal to confirm the allocation

steps.name: close_confirmation
steps.instruction: Click the 'OK' button to close the success message

ses_notification.description: Send email notification via AWS SES API
ses_notification.note: No browser action - uses AWS SES API directly

full_onboarding.description: Complete 8-phase IT onboarding workflow with conditional procurement

procurement_cycle.description: Complete procurement: create, submit, approve, receive

conditional_logic.stock_sufficient: Skips procurement phases (4a-4d) and inventory add (5) when stock_level >= required quantity
